subject: chore(directive): queue batch-20260504-193000 from modernization-pillar.prq.md
build(deps): migrate from npm to pnpm 10
decision: Add pnpm to package.json as packageManager and use action-setup@v4.
why: Pinning a single pnpm version eliminates "works on my machine" drift across contributors and CI; corepack auto-resolves it without a separate install step.

subject: build(deps): bump TypeScript to 6.0.3
decision: Drop baseUrl rather than silence with `ignoreDeprecations: "6.0"`.
why: ignoreDeprecations is a temporary escape hatch — paths resolve fine without baseUrl in modern TS, and we'd just have to revisit at the TS 7 bump.

subject: build(deps): bump Vite 8, Vitest 4, @vitest/browser 4
decision: Bump Vite + Vitest + provider in one commit, not three.
why: Vitest's browser provider API is tied to its core; bumping just Vite would have left a mismatched test runner mid-tree. Single coordinated bump means one verification pass instead of three with intermediate breakage.

subject: build(deps): bump React to 19.2 and framer-motion to 12
decision: Bundle framer-motion 12 in this commit, not as its own.
why: framer-motion 11 doesn't list React 19 as a peer; bumping React without framer-motion would have left an installable-but-unsupported config in CI's first green run on this commit.

subject: test(integration): drop quarantined wizard-dialogue test
decision: Delete rather than rewrite in this PR.
why: The drifted test mocks 8+ persistence/fetch APIs that the new dialogue-engine no longer touches; rewriting it to the new shape is its own multi-hour effort that doesn't belong in the modernization pillar's scope.

subject: feat(python): enforce maxStdout in the worker, demote main-thread to verifier
decision: 256-byte slack on the main-thread verifier.
why: The worker's truncation marker (`\n[output truncated — exceeded N bytes]`) is fixed-size with N as a varying integer. Allowing slack lets the marker land in-buffer without the verifier flagging a false cap-miss; smaller than the marker = false alarms, larger than the marker = real misses still caught.

subject: feat(python): instrument Pyodide cold-start with budget warning
decision: 8000ms as the warn threshold rather than 3000ms.
why: 3000ms is the mid-tier-laptop target; 8000ms is the Chromebook target. Warning at the laptop target would fire on legitimate Chromebook sessions, training people to ignore the signal. The warn fires only when even the Chromebook target is missed — that's the "needs investigation" line.

subject: docs(state): refresh STATE.md against modernization-pillar progress
decision: Update STATE.md mid-pillar instead of waiting for the final M6.2 pass.
why: Reviewers landing on the branch need an honest picture of what's already shipped — pretending STATE.md is unchanged would conflict with the doctrine that STATE.md is the source of truth for in-flight work.

subject: feat(grading): real acceptsUserInput counter via worker monkey-patch
decision: Counter is incremented even when input() raises EOFError.
why: `acceptsUserInput` is asking "did the student wire input() into their flow," not "did stdin actually have a line." A program that calls `input()` and then crashes because there's no input still demonstrates the construct.

subject: feat(grading): real functionCalled counter via sys.settrace
decision: Tracer is enabled only when trackFunctions is non-empty.
why: sys.settrace adds per-frame overhead even in the no-op path. Skipping `sys.settrace(<callable>)` and explicitly calling `sys.settrace(None)` when there are no names to track keeps the cost off the critical path for the 95%+ of lessons that don't need it.

subject: test(e2e): add @axe-core/playwright a11y suite
decision: WCAG 2.2 AA, not WCAG 2.1.
why: 2.2 AA is the current WCAG release (Oct 2023) and what enterprise rulesets and EU EAA compliance increasingly require. 2.1 AA is a strict subset, so any 2.2 AA pass is automatically 2.1 AA-compliant.

subject: test(e2e): add Playwright visual-regression baseline
decision: 1% pixel threshold rather than zero.
why: Subpixel font rendering shifts across Chromium versions. Zero-diff requires pinning the browser version, which we don't (Playwright bundles its own Chromium that updates per-Playwright-version). 1% absorbs font wobble; meaningful regressions (missing element, wrong color) blow past 1% by an order of magnitude.

subject: feat(content): three new lessons — lists, files, classes
decision: file-via-Pyodide-virtual-FS as the lesson-8 fixture rather than `tempfile`.
why: Pyodide's MEMFS persists for the worker's lifetime, so a `with open('note.txt', 'w')` followed by `with open('note.txt', 'r')` in the same snippet works deterministically. The student doesn't need to learn cleanup semantics on top of the file API.
